title: New photo released in unsolved 1997 homicide of a N.H. woman
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A new photo of Rosalie Miller has been released in hopes of finding witnesses for her 1997 homicide case.
Miller's body was found in early 1997 in Auburn, NH; her death was ruled as asphyxiation by ligature strangulation.
Officials seek public assistance, especially from those who saw Miller in Manchester or Auburn during December 1996.

title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, is charged with murder after allegedly shooting and killing Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, R.I.
Sousa allegedly crashed his vehicle in Swansea, Massachusetts, shortly after the shooting, killing two people in the incident.
Police found a pistol and ammunition in Sousa's car; he is in custody at Rhode Island Hospital recovering from injuries.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins need draft picks and prospects for potential trade of Blues' center Robert Thomas.
Robert Thomas, 26, has surpassed 80 points consecutively and ranks highly in assists.
Bruins must balance acquiring Thomas with preserving draft picks and young talent for future.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Maine fines Brooklyn couple $3,000 without admission of guilt for allegedly poisoning neighbor's trees in Rockport.
Settlement reached after Maine Board of Pesticides Control linked Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett to tree deaths.
Ruth Graham's family disappointed by lack of apology, proposes donation to Maine coastal preservation in her memory.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Texas A&M WR KC Concepcion impressed at the NFL combine, showcasing strong route-running with 919 yards and 9 touchdowns last season.
Texas Tech EDGE Romello Height posted strong athletic metrics, finishing in the top 10 in multiple drills, backing his productive 2025 season.
Arizona State OT Max Iheanachor demonstrated elite athleticism for his size, potentially making him a first-round target for the Patriots.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are unlikely to trade major draft capital for a star player this offseason, per ESPN's Mike Reiss.
They have shown interest in Eagles' A.J. Brown and Raiders' Maxx Crosby, but costs remain high for both players.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasized building through the draft, with New England holding 11 picks in the 2026 NFL Draft.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power received state approval to build Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage system on former ExxonMobil tank farm in Everett.
The facility will feature 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures, two substations, and two underground transmission lines connecting to Eversource.
The project is part of the first phase of Everett's "Docklands" mixed-use development, including future residential and commercial spaces.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on February 28, with musical guest Mumford & Sons.
Storrie's 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams appeared in a skating-themed sketch alongside him.
Members of the US Olympic hockey teams, including Hilary Knight, appeared during the opening monologue.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution lost 1-0 to the New York Red Bulls with a back-post finish from Julian Hall.
Brayan Ceballos returned as the Revolution's back line managed to resist Red Bulls' strong possession in the first half.
Despite improved second-half play, New England failed to capitalize on chances, resulting in a second consecutive defeat.

title: Triston Casas reportedly unlikely to be ready by Opening Day, while Kutter Crawford, Patrick Sandoval take step forward in rehab
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Triston Casas is unlikely to be ready for MLB action by Opening Day due to a torn patellar tendon.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval have started live batting practice and aim for readiness by the season's start.
Offseason acquisitions leave role uncertainties for Casas, Crawford, and Sandoval on the Red Sox's 2026 roster.
